The '''70th British Academy Film Awards''', more commonly known as the [[BAFTAs]], were held on 12 February 2017 at the [[Royal Albert Hall]] in [[London]], honouring the best national and foreign films of [[2016 in film|2016]]. Presented by the [[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]], accolades were handed out for the best feature-length film and documentaries of any nationality that were screened at British cinemas in 2016.

The nominees were announced on 10 January 2017 by actor [[Dominic Cooper]] and actress [[Sophie Turner]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.irishexaminer.com/breakingnews/entertainment/baftas-will-be-raucous-party-says-preacher-star-dominic-cooper-771846.html |title=Baftas will be raucous party, says Preacher star Dominic Cooper |date=10 January 2017 |work=[[Irish Examiner]] |access-date=10 January 2017}}</ref> ''[[La La Land]]'' received the most nominations in twelve categories; ''[[Arrival (film)|Arrival]]'' and ''[[Nocturnal Animals]]'' followed with nine each.<ref name="Nominations">{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/entertainment-arts-38567506 |title=La La Land leads Bafta film nominations |date=10 January 2017 |work=[[BBC News]] |access-date=10 January 2017}}</ref>

The ceremony was hosted by [[Stephen Fry]] for the twelfth time.<ref name="Host">{{cite web |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2016/nov/08/stephen-fry-host-baftas--12th-time-royal-festival-hall |title=Stephen Fry to host Baftas for 12th time |last=Evans |first=Alan |date=8 November 2016 |work=[[The Guardian]] |access-date=8 November 2016}}</ref>

The nominees were announced on 10 January 2017.<ref name="Nominations" /> The winners were announced on 12 February 2017.<ref name="Winners">{{cite web |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/films/2017/02/12/bafta-winners-2017-full-list-film-award-winners/ |title=Bafta winners 2017, full list: victory for La La Land and I, Daniel Blake |last=Saunders |first=Tristram Fane |date=13 February 2017 |work=[[The Daily Telegraph|The Telegraph]] |access-date=13 February 2017}}</ref>

==Ceremony information==
The ceremony was broadcast on [[BBC One]] at 9 p.m. [[Coordinated Universal Time|UTC]], around two hours later than the actual ceremony. For the 12th time, [[Stephen Fry]] acted as the host.<ref name="Host">{{cite web |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2016/nov/08/stephen-fry-host-baftas--12th-time-royal-festival-hall |title=Stephen Fry to host Baftas for 12th time |last=Evans |first=Alan |date=8 November 2016 |work=[[The Guardian]] |access-date=8 November 2016}}</ref> The ceremony returned to the [[Royal Albert Hall]] for the first time since 1997, as the [[Royal Opera House]], which has hosted the awards since 2008, was being refurbished.<ref name="Host" />

Following criticism at the lack of representation of ethnic minorities from the [[69th British Academy Film Awards|previous ceremony]], BAFTA had announced steps to increase diversity across the industry, on both sides of the camera.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/bafta-unveils-new-diversity-initiatives-film-awards-956027 |title=BAFTA Unveils New Diversity Initiatives for Film Awards |last=Ritman |first=Alex |date=14 December 2016 |work=[[The Hollywood Reporter]] |access-date=14 December 2016}}</ref><ref name="BAME">{{cite web |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2017/jan/11/bafta-race-row-awards-bame-nominations-diversity |title=Bafta race row: awards slammed for scarcity of BAME nominations |last=Pulver |first=Andrew |date=11 January 2017 |work=[[The Guardian]] |access-date=11 January 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2016/film/awards/bafta-diversity-british-filmmaking-1201941179/ |title=BAFTA Moves to Improve Diversity in British Filmmaking |last=Barraclough |first=Leo |date=14 December 2016 |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |access-date=14 December 2016}}</ref> However, there was again criticism at the lack of representation of [[Black people|Black]], [[Asian people|Asian]] and [[minority group|minority ethnic]] (BAME) actors, directors and films in the nominations. In the leading actor, actress and director fields, there were no BAME nominees, with the omission of director [[Barry Jenkins]] for ''[[Moonlight (2016 film)|Moonlight]]'' and actor/director [[Denzel Washington]] for ''[[Fences (film)|Fences]]'' highlighted as particularly noteworthy.<ref name="BAME" />

''[[La La Land]]'' won the most awards at the event, winning five—Best Picture, Best Director for [[Damien Chazelle]], Best Actress in a Leading Role for [[Emma Stone]], Best Cinematography for [[Linus Sandgren]], and Best Original Music for [[Justin Hurwitz]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/live/2017/feb/12/baftas-2017-live-the-arrivals-the-ceremony-the-speeches-and-the-frocks |title=The awards, the speeches and the frocks: Baftas 2017 - as it happened |last1=Heritage |last2=Marriott |last3=Cochrane |first1=Stuart |first2=Hannah |first3=Lauren |date=12 February 2017 |work=[[The Guardian]] |access-date=12 February 2017}}</ref> [[Casey Affleck]] won Best Actor in a Leading Role for ''[[Manchester by the Sea (film)|Manchester by the Sea]]'', [[Dev Patel]] won Best Actor in a Supporting Role for ''[[Lion (2016 film)|Lion]]'', and [[Viola Davis]] won Best Actress in a Supporting Role for ''[[Fences (film)|Fences]]''.<ref name="Winners" />

Cellist [[Sheku Kanneh-Mason]] performed a solo interpretation of [[Leonard Cohen]]'s "[[Hallelujah (Leonard Cohen song)|Hallelujah]]" to accompany the ''In Memoriam'' section.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.classicfm.com/discover-music/leonard-cohen-hallelujah-chamber/ |title=This cellist has rearranged Leonard Cohen's Hallelujah into a spine-tingling chamber masterpiece |date=13 February 2017 |publisher=[[Classic FM (UK)|Classic FM]] |access-date=13 February 2017}}</ref> Those remembered were [[Gene Wilder]], [[Garry Marshall]], [[Sue Gibson (cinematographer)|Sue Gibson]], [[Kenny Baker (English actor)|Kenny Baker]], [[Tony Dyson]], [[Peter Shaffer]], Paul Lewis, [[Michael White (producer)|Michael White]], [[Ken Adam]], [[Guy Hamilton]], [[Debbie Reynolds]], [[Carrie Fisher]], [[Abbas Kiarostami]], [[Jim Clark (film editor)|Jim Clark]], [[Simon Relph]], [[Douglas Slocombe]], [[Anton Yelchin]], [[Robin Hardy (film director)|Robin Hardy]], [[David Rose (producer)|David Rose]], [[Curtis Hanson]], Clare Wise, [[Om Puri]], [[Alec McCowen]], [[Emmanuelle Riva]], [[Andrzej Wajda]], [[Michael Cimino]], [[Antony Gibbs]] and [[John Hurt|Sir John Hurt]].
